description: Add E2E test coverage for the fetchViaBackground cross-origin cookie proxy. The entire chain (MAIN world CustomEvent -> ISOLATED world relay -> background service worker -> chrome.cookies.getAll -> cross-origin fetch -> response chain) currently has zero E2E coverage. This PRD adds a cross-origin test server, a test plugin tool exercising fetchViaBackground, and comprehensive E2E tests.

userStories.title: Add cross-origin test server and fetchViaBackground test tool to e2e-test plugin
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want a cross-origin test server and a test tool that exercises fetchViaBackground so that E2E tests can verify the full proxy chain.
acceptanceCriteria: A new cross-origin test server exists at e2e/cross-origin-test-server.ts that listens on an ephemeral port (PORT=0)

The cross-origin test server has POST /api/echo-with-cookies that returns JSON with: { cookies: Record<string,string>, body: <request body>, method: <request method> } so tests can verify cookies were attached
The cross-origin test server has GET /api/simple that returns { ok: true } for basic connectivity tests

The cross-origin test server has POST /control/set-cookie that sets an HttpOnly cookie on the response (for the test server's domain), and GET /control/cookies that returns all cookies set via /control/set-cookie

The cross-origin test server returns proper CORS headers: Access-Control-Allow-Origin matching the requesting origin, Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true, Access-Control-Allow-Headers listing Content-Type and any custom headers
A Playwright fixture crossOriginServer is added to e2e/fixtures.ts that starts the cross-origin test server
A new tool sdk_fetch_via_background is added to the e2e-test plugin that calls context.fetchViaBackground(url, init) with the provided url and init, returning the FetchProxyResponse
A new tool sdk_fetch_via_background_concurrent is added that makes N parallel fetchViaBackground calls and returns all responses
The e2e-test plugin passes build, type-check, lint, format:check
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to create/modify:

1. `e2e/cross-origin-test-server.ts` — model after `e2e/test-server.ts` but much simpler. Key endpoints:
   - `POST /api/echo-with-cookies` — reads Cookie header from request, returns `{ cookies: parsedCookies, body: requestBody, method: 'POST', headers: requestHeaders }`
   - `GET /api/simple` — returns `{ ok: true }`
   - `POST /control/set-cookie` — body `{ name, value, httpOnly?, sameSite? }` — sets a Set-Cookie header on the response
   - CORS: respond to OPTIONS preflight with `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: <origin>`, `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true`, `Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type, useraccount, linear-client-id`
   - Start with `PORT=0` and print the URL to stdout (same pattern as test-server.ts)

2. `e2e/fixtures.ts` — add a `crossOriginServer` fixture:
   - Uses `startServerProcess()` with `e2e/cross-origin-test-server.ts`
   - Provides the server URL to tests
   - Also sets a cookie on the Chromium browser context for the cross-origin server's URL using `browserContext.addCookies()` (to simulate HttpOnly session cookies)

3. `plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/sdk-fetch-via-background.ts` — new tool:
   ```typescript
   export const sdkFetchViaBackground = defineTool({
     name: 'sdk_fetch_via_background',
     input: z.object({ url: z.string(), method: z.string().optional(), headers: z.record(z.string()).optional(), body: z.string().optional() }),
     output: z.object({ status: z.number(), statusText: z.string(), headers: z.record(z.string()), body: z.string(), ok: z.boolean() }),
     handle: async (params, context) => {
       if (!context?.fetchViaBackground) throw ToolError.internal('fetchViaBackground not available');
       const response = await context.fetchViaBackground(params.url, { method: params.method ?? 'GET', headers: params.headers, body: params.body });
       return response;
     },
   });
   ```

4. `plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/sdk-fetch-via-background-concurrent.ts` — new tool:
   - Takes `{ urls: string[], method?: string, headers?: Record<string,string> }`
   - Makes parallel `context.fetchViaBackground()` calls for each URL
   - Returns `{ responses: FetchProxyResponse[] }`

5. `plugins/e2e-test/src/index.ts` — register the two new tools

Important: The e2e-test plugin's urlPatterns is `['http://localhost/*']`. The cross-origin test server runs on a different port but same hostname (localhost). The fetchViaBackground domain validation checks registrable domain, so `localhost` -> `localhost` will pass. If using `127.0.0.1` as the cross-origin host, ensure the e2e-test plugin's urlPatterns include it or use localhost for both (just different ports). Different ports on the same hostname ARE cross-origin for CORS but share the same registrable domain for the security check.

The test harness should use `context.addCookies()` from Playwright to set HttpOnly cookies on the cross-origin server's URL before running tests. This simulates how a real web app (like Linear) has HttpOnly session cookies set by the server.

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for fetchViaBackground cross-origin proxy
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want comprehensive E2E tests for fetchViaBackground so that the full proxy chain is verified against a real Chrome extension.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file e2e/sdk-fetch-via-background.e2e.ts exists
Test: basic cross-origin fetch — calls sdk_fetch_via_background with a cross-origin URL, verifies the response status, body, and ok field

Test: cookies attached — sets HttpOnly cookies via Playwright context.addCookies() for the cross-origin server, calls sdk_fetch_via_background, verifies the cross-origin server received the cookies in the request
Test: custom headers forwarded — passes custom headers (e.g. useraccount, Content-Type), verifies the cross-origin server received them
Test: POST with body — sends a POST with JSON body, verifies the cross-origin server received the body

Test: domain validation — calls sdk_fetch_via_background with a URL on a domain that does NOT match the plugin's urlPatterns (e.g. https://evil.example.com), verifies the call is rejected with an error
Test: concurrent requests — calls sdk_fetch_via_background_concurrent with multiple URLs, verifies all responses are received correctly
Test: network error handling — calls sdk_fetch_via_background with an unreachable URL, verifies the error is propagated
All existing E2E tests continue to pass
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
